CheckTitle: AI Agent Identity Must Be Registered Across All Target Cloud Providers
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Identity**. Ensures that multi-cloud AI agents have a consistent, federated identity registered in every target cloud provider. Without cross-cloud registration agents fall back to static credentials for inter-cloud authentication, creating long-lived secret exposure.
Risk: Agents that are not registered across clouds must use static API keys or access tokens to authenticate to secondary cloud providers. These long-lived credentials are a high-value target for attackers and are difficult to rotate automatically.

Code.Other:
1. Configure OIDC Workload Identity Federation between the primary cloud and each secondary cloud.
2. For AWS-to-GCP: set up GCP Workload Identity Pool with the AWS IAM OIDC provider.
3. For AWS-to-Azure: configure Azure AD Federated Identity Credentials trusting the AWS IAM OIDC issuer.
4. Remove static cross-cloud credentials once federation is established.

Recommendation.Text: Use OIDC Workload Identity Federation to provide agents with short-lived tokens for each cloud provider, eliminating the need for static cross-cloud credentials.

Notes: ASPM-003 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
